name: "Stop losing ideas between three apps"
description: Consolidation angle — pitches Acme Notes as the replacement for the Apple Notes + Notion + Google Docs pile most people run
notes: Hero candidate; could pair with comparison logos

name: "Fewer clicks, more thinking"
description: UX slogan angle — positions speed and low-friction as the core value. Works as subhead, short tweet, or ad headline
notes: Generic; used mostly as supporting line, not hero

name: "Your meeting notes write themselves"
description: Flagship AI-summarization angle. Works as homepage hero, 60-sec demo title, and YouTube thumbnail. Highest-gravity angle in the catalog
notes: Backed by AI summarization + auto-tag shipping

name: "Search that finds what you meant, not what you typed"
description: Semantic search angle — the "magic" moment framed as a promise. Reads as a tweet or landing line
notes: Relies on semantic search being tuned

name: "Import your Evernote in one click"
description: Direct migration angle — wedge against the incumbent. Cold-email subject line candidate; landing section under "switching is easy"

name: "The note app that doesn't lock you in"
description: Export-freedom angle. Trust signal for the landing page, blog-post intro, Hacker News framing

name: "Real-time collab without the Google Docs bloat"
description: Direct competitor framing. Works as features-grid headline, comparison-page header, X thread title

name: "Your second brain, searchable and shareable"
description: Knowledge-management angle. Pitches the product to power users and PKM audience on X / Hacker News

name: "Brain-dump → organized in 15 seconds"
description: Before/after transformation angle. Best-suited for demo video, animated GIF, tweet with video
notes: Needs a clean 15-sec capture; high-virality potential

name: "Notion, but for people who actually take notes"
description: Snarkier comparison angle. Works best as tweet or LinkedIn hook; probably too sharp for homepage hero
notes: Risk: combative; fits guerrilla marketing, not brand page

name: "Works offline, syncs when you're back"
description: Reliability angle. Trust-signal line for landing, sales talking point, enterprise-pitch de-risking
notes: Offline-first is a core shipped feature

name: "Templates for every meeting type"
description: Low-friction onboarding angle. Features-grid line, blog SEO post candidate ("best meeting note templates")

name: "The note app your entire team actually uses"
description: Team-adoption angle. Sales-pitch line, LinkedIn post for managers
notes: Depends on collab rollout; team adoption still uneven

name: "Save any Slack message as a note with one emoji"
description: Slack-reaction integration angle. Demo clip, tweet hook, Slack-App-Directory listing headline

name: "Calendar meetings auto-become notes"
description: Calendar-sync integration angle. Sales pitch for PMs and execs; LinkedIn post candidate

name: "Backlinks turn your notes into a personal wiki"
description: Backlinks/PKM angle. Blog SEO, X post for Obsidian/Roam audience

name: "Comment on any paragraph, get your team's eyes"
description: Team-collab micro-angle. Sales-pitch line; features-grid bullet

name: "The note app that actually remembers"
description: Memory hook — pitches semantic search and auto-recall as the product's personality. Strong as hero and blog headline
notes: Strong AI-era candidate despite NOW horizon

name: "Everything exports to markdown, nothing held hostage"
description: Portability angle. Trust signal for landing footer, Hacker News comment, enterprise pitch

name: "Widget on your home screen, notes one tap away"
description: Mobile quick-capture angle. App Store screenshot caption, Product Hunt tagline

name: "Write with a pen, search in plain text"
description: Handwriting → text angle. Apple Pencil / iPad audience. Blog-post SEO

name: "Single sign-on and admin controls for your team"
description: Enterprise-readiness angle. Features-grid line for /enterprise page; procurement de-risking bullet

name: "Audit log every admin needs to sleep at night"
description: Compliance signal angle. Enterprise-page bullet; compliance landing section

name: "Your notes become your task list automatically"
description: AI task-extraction angle (FUTURE). Vision / "coming soon" section on landing; a strong narrative for a roadmap-revealing blog post
notes: Unbuilt. Scores high under AI era — strong candidate to build toward

name: "Capture it in your voice, record it in your voice"
description: Voice capture angle. Podcast-pitch tagline; mobile-widget subtext

name: "Your second brain, on your wrist"
description: Apple Watch capture angle (FUTURE). Viral tweet candidate; hardware-showcase post. Unbuilt but strong AI-era score
notes: Unbuilt. Pair with AI task extraction for full story

name: "Local-first. Encrypted. Synced only if you want it."
description: Privacy-first angle (FUTURE). Hacker News / Privacy-community audience. Contrarian vs cloud-first competitors
notes: Unbuilt. Differentiation-heavy; appeals to niche but vocal audience

name: "Your meeting happens. Your notes exist."
description: Fully-automated capture angle (FUTURE). Ambitious hero / keynote tagline. Requires calendar + audio + AI summarization stack
notes: Unbuilt. Pair with task-extraction angle for a full "your brain, automated" vision section
